This module defines the result types used by the YAML parser.\n4\t//! This module also integrates error types from bf-68hqo for comprehensive\n5\t//! error categorization and handling.\n6\t\n7\tuse crate::parsers::yaml::error::{ParseError, Result};\n8\tuse std::fmt;\n9\t\n10\t/// Error type categorization for validation and parsing errors\n11\t///\n12\t/// This enum defines the high-level categories of errors that can occur\n13\t/// during YAML processing and schema validation, following the bf-68hqo\n14\t/// error hierarchy design.\n15\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n16\tpub enum ErrorType {\n17\t    /// Syntax errors in YAML source (invalid structure, malformed tokens)\n18\t    Syntax,\n19\t    /// I/O errors (file not found, permission denied, read failures)\n20\t    Io,\n21\t    /// Validation errors (constraint violations, required field missing)\n22\t    Validation,\n23\t    /// Type mismatch errors (unexpected type for a field)\n24\t    TypeMismatch,\n25\t    /// UTF-8 encoding errors\n26\t    InvalidUtf8,\n27\t    /// Unexpected end of input\n28\t    UnexpectedEof,\n29\t    /// Unknown anchor or alias references\n30\t    UnknownAnchor,\n31\t    /// Duplicate key in mapping\n32\t    DuplicateKey,\n33\t    /// Other uncategorized errors\n34\t    Other,\n35\t}\n36\t\n37\timpl fmt::Display for ErrorType {\n38\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n39\t        match self {\n40\t            Self::Syntax => write!(f, \"syntax error\"),\n41\t            Self::Io => write!(f, \"I/O error\"),\n42\t            Self::Validation => write!(f, \"validation error\"),\n43\t            Self::TypeMismatch => write!(f, \"type mismatch\"),\n44\t            Self::InvalidUtf8 => write!(f, \"invalid UTF-8 encoding\"),\n45\t            Self::UnexpectedEof => write!(f, \"unexpected end of input\"),\n46\t            Self::UnknownAnchor => write!(f, \"unknown anchor\"),\n47\t            Self::DuplicateKey => write!(f, \"duplicate key\"),\n48\t            Self::Other => write!(f, \"error\"),\n49\t        }\n50\t    }\n51\t}\n52\t\n53\t/// Specific error codes for programmatic error handling\n54\t///\n55\t/// These codes provide machine-readable identifiers for specific error conditions,\n56\t/// enabling automated error handling and reporting. Following the bf-68hqo design,\n57\t/// each code maps to a specific validation or parsing failure scenario.\n58\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n59\t#[allow(non_camel_case_types)]\n60\tpub enum ErrorCode {\n61\t    // ========== Syntax Errors ==========\n62\t    /// Invalid YAML syntax structure\n63\t    YAML_INVALID_SYNTAX,\n64\t    /// Invalid indentation in YAML\n65\t    YAML_INVALID_INDENTATION,\n66\t    /// Invalid delimiter (colon, bracket, brace)\n67\t    YAML_INVALID_DELIMITER,\n68\t    /// Invalid escape sequence\n69\t    YAML_INVALID_ESCAPE_SEQUENCE,\n70\t    /// Invalid scalar value\n71\t    YAML_INVALID_SCALAR,\n72\t\n73\t    // ========== Type Mismatches ==========\n74\t    /// Expected integer, got another type\n75\t    TYPE_EXPECTED_INTEGER,\n76\t    /// Expected string, got another type\n77\t    TYPE_EXPECTED_STRING,\n78\t    /// Expected boolean, got another type\n79\t    TYPE_EXPECTED_BOOLEAN,\n80\t    /// Expected array, got another type\n81\t    TYPE_EXPECTED_ARRAY,\n82\t    /// Expected object/map, got another type\n83\t    TYPE_EXPECTED_OBJECT,\n84\t    /// Expected number, got another type\n85\t    TYPE_EXPECTED_NUMBER,\n86\t    /// Null value where non-null required\n87\t    TYPE_UNEXPECTED_NULL,\n88\t\n89\t    // ========== Validation Errors ==========\n90\t    /// Required field is missing\n91\t    VALIDATION_REQUIRED_FIELD_MISSING,\n92\t    /// Value out of allowed range\n93\t    VALIDATION_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE,\n94\t    /// String too short\n95\t    VALIDATION_STRING_TOO_SHORT,\n96\t    /// String too long\n97\t    VALIDATION_STRING_TOO_LONG,\n98\t    /// String doesn't match pattern\n99\t    VALIDATION_PATTERN_MISMATCH,\n100\t    /// Value not in allowed set\n101\t    VALIDATION_INVALID_VALUE,\n102\t    /// Array has too few items\n103\t    VALIDATION_ARRAY_TOO_FEW_ITEMS,\n104\t    /// Array has too many items\n105\t    VALIDATION_ARRAY_TOO_MANY_ITEMS,\n106\t    /// Array items not unique\n107\t    VALIDATION_ARRAY_NOT_UNIQUE,\n108\t    /// Object has too few properties\n109\t    VALIDATION_OBJECT_TOO_FEW_PROPERTIES,\n110\t    /// Object has too many properties\n111\t    VALIDATION_OBJECT_TOO_MANY_PROPERTIES,\n112\t\n113\t    // ========== I/O Errors ==========\n114\t    /// File not found\n115\t    IO_FILE_NOT_FOUND,\n116\t    /// Permission denied\n117\t    IO_PERMISSION_DENIED,\n118\t    /// Read failed\n119\t    IO_READ_FAILED,\n120\t    /// Write failed\n121\t    IO_WRITE_FAILED,\n122\t\n123\t    // ========== Encoding Errors ==========\n124\t    /// Invalid UTF-8 sequence\n125\t    ENCODING_INVALID_UTF8,\n126\t\n127\t    // ========== Other Errors ==========\n128\t    /// Unknown anchor reference\n129\t    ANCHOR_UNKNOWN,\n130\t    /// Duplicate key in mapping\n131\t    KEY_DUPLICATE,\n132\t    /// Unexpected end of input\n133\t    EOF_UNEXPECTED,\n134\t}\n135\t\n136\timpl ErrorCode {\n137\t    /// Get the error type category for this error code\n138\t    pub fn error_type(&self) -> ErrorType {\n139\t        match self {\n140\t            // Syntax errors\n141\t            Self::YAML_INVALID_SYNTAX | Self::YAML_INVALID_INDENTATION |\n142\t            Self::YAML_INVALID_DELIMITER | Self::YAML_INVALID_ESCAPE_SEQUENCE |\n143\t            Self::YAML_INVALID_SCALAR => ErrorType::Syntax,\n144\t\n145\t            // Type mismatches\n146\t            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_INTEGER | Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_STRING |\n147\t            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_BOOLEAN | Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_ARRAY |\n148\t            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_OBJECT | Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_NUMBER |\n149\t            Self::TYPE_UNEXPECTED_NULL => ErrorType::TypeMismatch,\n150\t\n151\t            // Validation errors\n152\t            Self::VALIDATION_REQUIRED_FIELD_MISSING | Self::VALIDATION_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE |\n153\t            Self::VALIDATION_STRING_TOO_SHORT | Self::VALIDATION_STRING_TOO_LONG |\n154\t            Self::VALIDATION_PATTERN_MISMATCH | Self::VALIDATION_INVALID_VALUE |\n155\t            Self::VALIDATION_ARRAY_TOO_FEW_ITEMS | Self::VALIDATION_ARRAY_TOO_MANY_ITEMS |\n156\t            Self::VALIDATION_ARRAY_NOT_UNIQUE | Self::VALIDATION_OBJECT_TOO_FEW_PROPERTIES |\n157\t            Self::VALIDATION_OBJECT_TOO_MANY_PROPERTIES => ErrorType::Validation,\n158\t\n159\t            // I/O errors\n160\t            Self::IO_FILE_NOT_FOUND | Self::IO_PERMISSION_DENIED |\n161\t            Self::IO_READ_FAILED | Self::IO_WRITE_FAILED => ErrorType::Io,\n162\t\n163\t            // Encoding errors\n164\t            Self::ENCODING_INVALID_UTF8 => ErrorType::InvalidUtf8,\n165\t\n166\t            // Other errors\n167\t            Self::ANCHOR_UNKNOWN => ErrorType::UnknownAnchor,\n168\t            Self::KEY_DUPLICATE => ErrorType::DuplicateKey,\n169\t            Self::EOF_UNEXPECTED => ErrorType::UnexpectedEof,\n170\t        }\n171\t    }\n172\t\n173\t    /// Get a human-readable description for this error code\n174\t    pub fn description(&self) -> &'static str {\n175\t        match self {\n176\t            // Syntax errors\n177\t            Self::YAML_INVALID_SYNTAX => \"Invalid YAML syntax structure\",\n178\t            Self::YAML_INVALID_INDENTATION => \"Invalid YAML indentation\",\n179\t            Self::YAML_INVALID_DELIMITER => \"Invalid YAML delimiter\",\n180\t            Self::YAML_INVALID_ESCAPE_SEQUENCE => \"Invalid escape sequence\",\n181\t            Self::YAML_INVALID_SCALAR => \"Invalid scalar value\",\n182\t\n183\t            // Type mismatches\n184\t            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_INTEGER => \"Expected integer type\",\n185\t            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_STRING => \"Expected string type\",\n186\t            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_BOOLEAN => \"Expected boolean type\",\n187\t            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_ARRAY => \"Expected array type\",\n188\t            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_OBJECT => \"Expected object type\",\n189\t            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_NUMBER => \"Expected number type\",\n190\t            Self::TYPE_UNEXPECTED_NULL => \"Unexpected null value\",\n191\t\n192\t            // Validation errors\n193\t            Self::VALIDATION_REQUIRED_FIELD_MISSING => \"Required field is missing\",\n194\t            Self::VALIDATION_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE => \"Value out of allowed range\",\n195\t            Self::VALIDATION_STRING_TOO_SHORT => \"String too short\",\n196\t            Self::VALIDATION_STRING_TOO_LONG => \"String too long\",\n197\t            Self::VALIDATION_PATTERN_MISMATCH => \"Pattern mismatch\",\n198\t            Self::VALIDATION_INVALID_VALUE => \"Invalid value\",\n199\t            Self::VALIDATION_ARRAY_TOO_FEW_ITEMS => \"Array has too few items\",\n200\t            Self::VALIDATION_ARRAY_TOO_MANY_ITEMS => \"Array has too many items\",\n201\t            Self::VALIDATION_ARRAY_NOT_UNIQUE => \"Array items not unique\",\n202\t            Self::VALIDATION_OBJECT_TOO_FEW_PROPERTIES => \"Object has too few properties\",\n203\t            Self::VALIDATION_OBJECT_TOO_MANY_PROPERTIES => \"Object has too many properties\",\n204\t\n205\t            // I/O errors\n206\t            Self::IO_FILE_NOT_FOUND => \"File not found\",\n207\t            Self::IO_PERMISSION_DENIED => \"Permission denied\",\n208\t            Self::IO_READ_FAILED => \"Read failed\",\n209\t            Self::IO_WRITE_FAILED => \"Write failed\",\n210\t\n211\t            // Encoding errors\n212\t            Self::ENCODING_INVALID_UTF8 => \"Invalid UTF-8 encoding\",\n213\t\n214\t            // Other errors\n215\t            Self::ANCHOR_UNKNOWN => \"Unknown anchor reference\",\n216\t            Self::KEY_DUPLICATE => \"Duplicate key in mapping\",\n217\t            Self::EOF_UNEXPECTED => \"Unexpected end of input\",\n218\t        }\n219\t    }\n220\t}\n221\t\n222\timpl fmt::Display for ErrorCode {\n223\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n224\t        write!(f, \"{:?}\", self)\n225\t    }\n226\t}\n227\t\n228\t/// Status enum representing success/error states for Result types\n229\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n230\tpub enum Status {\n231\t    /// Operation completed successfully\n232\t    SUCCESS,\n233\t    /// Operation encountered an error\n234\t    ERROR,\n235\t}\n236\t\n237\timpl Status {\n238\t    /// Check if status is SUCCESS\n239\t    pub fn is_success(self) -> bool {\n240\t        matches!(self, Status::SUCCESS)\n241\t    }\n242\t\n243\t    /// Check if status is ERROR\n244\t    pub fn is_error(self) -> bool {\n245\t        matches!(self, Status::ERROR)\n246\t    }\n247\t\n248\t    /// Convert from boolean (true = SUCCESS, false = ERROR)\n249\t    pub fn from_bool(success: bool) -> Self {\n250\t        if success {\n251\t            Status::SUCCESS\n252\t        } else {\n253\t            Status::ERROR\n254\t        }\n255\t    }\n256\t\n257\t    /// Convert to boolean\n258\t    pub fn as_bool(self) -> bool {\n259\t        self.is_success()\n260\t    }\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\timpl fmt::Display for Status {\n264\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n265\t        match self {\n266\t            Status::SUCCESS => write!(f, \"SUCCESS\"),\n267\t            Status::ERROR => write!(f, \"ERROR\"),\n268\t        }\n269\t    }\n270\t}\n271\t\n272\t/// Result of a YAML operation with status, data, and error fields\n273\t///\n274\t/// This is a simple dataclass-style result type that holds:\n275\t/// - `status`: A Status enum indicating SUCCESS or ERROR\n276\t/// - `data`: Optional generic data payload\n277\t/// - `error`: Optional error message string\n278\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n279\tpub struct OperationResult<T> {\n280\t    /// The operation status (SUCCESS or ERROR)\n281\t    pub status: Status,\n282\t    /// The parsed content data, if successful\n283\t    pub data: Option<T>,\n284\t    /// The error message, if the operation failed\n285\t    pub error: Option<String>,\n286\t}\n287\t\n288\timpl<T> OperationResult<T> {\n289\t    /// Create a new OperationResult with all three fields\n290\t    ///\n291\t    /// # Arguments\n292\t    /// * `status` - The Status enum value (SUCCESS or ERROR)\n293\t    /// * `data` - Optional data payload\n294\t    /// * `error` - Optional error message\n295\t    pub fn new(status: Status, data: Option<T>, error: Option<String>) -> Self {\n296\t        Self { status, data, error }\n297\t    }\n298\t\n299\t    /// Create a successful OperationResult with data\n300\t    ///\n301\t    /// # Arguments\n302\t    /// * `data` - The successful result data\n303\t    pub fn success(data: T) -> Self {\n304\t        Self {\n305\t            status: Status::SUCCESS,\n306\t            data: Some(data),\n307\t            error: None,\n308\t        }\n309\t    }\n310\t\n311\t    /// Create a failed OperationResult with an error message\n312\t    ///\n313\t    /// # Arguments\n314\t    /// * `message` - The error message describing the failure\n315\t    pub fn error(message: String) -> Self {\n316\t        Self {\n317\t            status: Status::ERROR,\n318\t            data: None,\n319\t            error: Some(message),\n320\t        }\n321\t    }\n322\t\n323\t    /// Check if the operation was successful\n324\t    pub fn is_success(&self) -> bool {\n325\t        self.status.is_success()\n326\t    }\n327\t\n328\t    /// Check if the operation failed\n329\t    pub fn is_error(&self) -> bool {\n330\t        self.status.is_error()\n331\t    }\n332\t\n333\t    /// Get a reference to the data, if successful\n334\t    pub fn get_data(&self) -> Option<&T> {\n335\t        self.data.as_ref()\n336\t    }\n337\t\n338\t    /// Get the error message as a string slice, if failed\n339\t    pub fn get_error(&self) -> Option<&str> {\n340\t        self.error.as_deref()\n341\t    }\n342\t}\n343\t\n344\t/// Result of a YAML parsing operation\n345\t///\n346\t/// `ParseResult<T>` represents the outcome of a YAML parsing operation, encapsulating\n347\t/// either a successful parse with its associated data and metadata, or a failure with\n348\t/// detailed error information.\n349\t///\n350\t/// # Type Parameter\n351\t///\n352\t/// - `T` - The type of the parsed value. This is typically the target type that the YAML\n353\t///   content is being parsed into (e.g., a configuration struct, `serde_yaml::Value`,\n354\t///   or any deserializable type).\n355\t///\n356\t/// # Fields\n357\t///\n358\t/// ## Core Fields\n359\t///\n360\t/// - `value: Option<T>` - The successfully parsed value. Present when parsing succeeds.\n361\t/// - `error: Option<ParseError>` - Detailed error information. Present when parsing fails.\n362\t/// - `metadata: ParseMetadata` - Metadata about the parsing operation (lines processed,\n363\t///   bytes processed, processing time, source path). Always present.\n364\t/// - `warnings: Vec<ParseWarning>` - Collection of non-fatal warnings that occurred during\n365\t///   parsing. Parsing can succeed with warnings present.\n366\t///\n367\t/// # Design Philosophy\n368\t///\n369\t/// `ParseResult<T>` follows a structured approach to representing parsing outcomes:\n370\t///\n371\t/// 1. **Explicit success/failure states** - Success requires both `value` present and\n372\t///    `error` absent. Failure requires `error` present and `value` absent.\n373\t/// 2. **Rich context** - Both success and failure carry metadata about the operation.\n374\t/// 3. **Warnings without failure** - Warnings are non-fatal; parsing can succeed with\n375\t///    warnings present (e.g., deprecated field usage, unknown keys in lenient mode).\n376\t/// 4. **Composable operations** - Supports `map()` for transforming success values,\n377\t///    and `From<Result<T>>` for interoperability with standard `Result` types.\n378\t///\n379\t/// # Examples\n380\t///\n381\t/// ## Successful Parse\n382\t///\n383\t/// ```ignore\n384\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseMetadata};\n385\t///\n386\t/// let result = ParseResult::success(42);\n387\t/// assert!(result.is_success());\n388\t/// assert_eq!(result.value(), Some(&42));\n389\t/// assert!(result.warnings().is_empty());\n390\t/// ```\n391\t///\n392\t/// ## Failed Parse\n393\t///\n394\t/// ```ignore\n395\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseError};\n396\t///\n397\t/// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\");\n398\t/// let result = ParseResult::<i32>::failure(error);\n399\t/// assert!(result.is_failure());\n400\t/// assert!(result.error().is_some());\n401\t/// ```\n402\t///\n403\t/// ## Parse with Warnings\n404\t///\n405\t/// ```ignore\n406\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseWarning};\n407\t///\n408\t/// let mut result = ParseResult::success(42);\n409\t/// result.add_warning(ParseWarning::deprecated_field(\"old_field\", \"new_field\"));\n410\t///\n411\t/// assert!(result.is_success());\n412\t/// assert!(!result.warnings().is_empty());\n413\t/// assert_eq!(result.warnings().len(), 1);\n414\t/// ```\n415\t///\n416\t/// ## Mapping Over Success\n417\t///\n418\t/// ```ignore\n419\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n420\t///\n421\t/// let result: ParseResult<i32> = ParseResult::success(10);\n422\t/// let mapped = result.map(|x| x * 2);\n423\t///\n424\t/// assert_eq!(mapped.value(), Some(&20));\n425\t/// ```\n426\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n427\tpub struct ParseResult<T> {\n428\t    /// The parsed value, if successful\n429\t    value: Option<T>,\n430\t    /// The error, if parsing failed\n431\t    error: Option<ParseError>,\n432\t    /// Additional metadata about the parse operation\n433\t    metadata: ParseMetadata,\n434\t    /// Non-fatal warnings that occurred during parsing\n435\t    warnings: Vec<ParseWarning>,\n436\t}\n437\t\n438\timpl<T> ParseResult<T> {\n439\t    /// Create a successful parse result\n440\t    ///\n441\t    /// # Arguments\n442\t    /// * `value` - The successfully parsed value\n443\t    ///\n444\t    /// # Returns\n445\t    /// A `ParseResult` with the value set, no error, empty warnings, and default metadata\n446\t    ///\n447\t    /// # Examples\n448\t    ///\n449\t    /// ```\n450\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n451\t    ///\n452\t    /// let result = ParseResult::success(42);\n453\t    /// assert!(result.is_success());\n454\t    /// assert_eq!(result.value(), Some(&42));\n455\t    /// ```\n456\t    pub fn success(value: T) -> Self {\n457\t        Self {\n458\t            value: Some(value),\n459\t            error: None,\n460\t            metadata: ParseMetadata::default(),\n461\t            warnings: Vec::new(),\n462\t        }\n463\t    }\n464\t\n465\t    /// Create a failed parse result\n466\t    ///\n467\t    /// # Arguments\n468\t    /// * `error` - The error that caused parsing to fail\n469\t    ///\n470\t    /// # Returns\n471\t    /// A `ParseResult` with the error set, no value, empty warnings, and default metadata\n472\t    ///\n473\t    /// # Examples\n474\t    ///\n475\t    /// ```\n476\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseError};\n477\t    ///\n478\t    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\");\n479\t    /// let result = ParseResult::<i32>::failure(error);\n480\t    /// assert!(result.is_failure());\n481\t    /// assert!(result.error().is_some());\n482\t    /// ```\n483\t    pub fn failure(error: ParseError) -> Self {\n484\t        Self {\n485\t            value: None,\n486\t            error: Some(error),\n487\t            metadata: ParseMetadata::default(),\n488\t            warnings: Vec::new(),\n489\t        }\n490\t    }\n491\t\n492\t    /// Check if the parse was successful\n493\t    pub fn is_success(&self) -> bool {\n494\t        self.error.is_none() && self.value.is_some()\n495\t    }\n496\t\n497\t    /// Check if the parse failed\n498\t    pub fn is_failure(&self) -> bool {\n499\t        self.error.is_some()\n500\t    }\n501\t\n502\t    /// Get the parsed value\n503\t    ///\n504\t    /// Returns None if the parse failed\n505\t    pub fn value(&self) -> Option<&T> {\n506\t        self.value.as_ref()\n507\t    }\n508\t\n509\t    /// Get the error, if any\n510\t    pub fn error(&self) -> Option<&ParseError> {\n511\t        self.error.as_ref()\n512\t    }\n513\t\n514\t    /// Get the metadata for this parse result\n515\t    ///\n516\t    /// # Returns\n517\t    /// A reference to the `ParseMetadata` containing information about lines processed,\n518\t    /// bytes processed, processing time, and source path\n519\t    pub fn metadata(&self) -> &ParseMetadata {\n520\t        &self.metadata\n521\t    }\n522\t\n523\t    /// Get the warnings for this parse result\n524\t    ///\n525\t    /// # Returns\n526\t    /// A slice of `ParseWarning` items representing non-fatal issues that occurred during parsing\n527\t    ///\n528\t    /// # Examples\n529\t    ///\n530\t    /// ```\n531\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseWarning};\n532\t    ///\n533\t    /// let mut result = ParseResult::success(42);\n534\t    /// result.add_warning(ParseWarning::deprecated_field(\"old\", \"new\"));\n535\t    ///\n536\t    /// assert_eq!(result.warnings().len(), 1);\n537\t    /// ```\n538\t    pub fn warnings(&self) -> &[ParseWarning] {\n539\t        &self.warnings\n540\t    }\n541\t\n542\t    /// Check if this parse result has any warnings\n543\t    ///\n544\t    /// # Returns\n545\t    /// `true` if there are warnings present, `false` otherwise\n546\t    pub fn has_warnings(&self) -> bool {\n547\t        !self.warnings.is_empty()\n548\t    }\n549\t\n550\t    /// Add a warning to this parse result\n551\t    ///\n552\t    /// # Arguments\n553\t    /// * `warning` - The warning to add\n554\t    ///\n555\t    /// # Examples\n556\t    ///\n557\t    /// ```\n558\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseWarning};\n559\t    ///\n560\t    /// let mut result = ParseResult::success(42);\n561\t    /// result.add_warning(ParseWarning::unknown_key(\"deprecated_field\"));\n562\t    /// assert!(result.has_warnings());\n563\t    /// ```\n564\t    pub fn add_warning(&mut self, warning: ParseWarning) {\n565\t        self.warnings.push(warning);\n566\t    }\n567\t\n568\t    /// Add warnings to this parse result\n569\t    ///\n570\t    /// # Arguments\n571\t    /// * `warnings` - An iterator of warnings to add\n572\t    pub fn add_warnings<I>(&mut self, warnings: I)\n573\t    where\n574\t        I: IntoIterator<Item = ParseWarning>,\n575\t    {\n576\t        self.warnings.extend(warnings);\n577\t    }\n578\t\n579\t    /// Set the metadata for this parse result\n580\t    ///\n581\t    /// # Arguments\n582\t    /// * `metadata` - The metadata to set\n583\t    pub fn with_metadata(mut self, metadata: ParseMetadata) -> Self {\n584\t        self.metadata = metadata;\n585\t        self\n586\t    }\n587\t\n588\t    /// Unwrap the value, consuming the result\n589\t    ///\n590\t    /// # Panics\n591\t    /// Panics if the parse failed (i.e., if `error` is present)\n592\t    ///\n593\t    /// # Examples\n594\t    ///\n595\t    /// ```\n596\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n597\t    ///\n598\t    /// let result = ParseResult::success(42);\n599\t    /// assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), 42);\n600\t    /// ```\n601\t    pub fn unwrap(self) -> T {\n602\t        self.value.expect(\n603\t            \"called unwrap() on a failed ParseResult\"\n604\t        )\n605\t    }\n606\t\n607\t    /// Unwrap the value or return a default\n608\t    ///\n609\t    /// # Arguments\n610\t    /// * `default` - The default value to return if parsing failed\n611\t    ///\n612\t    /// # Returns\n613\t    /// The parsed value if successful, or the provided default\n614\t    ///\n615\t    /// # Examples\n616\t    ///\n617\t    /// ```\n618\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseError};\n619\t    ///\n620\t    /// let success: ParseResult<i32> = ParseResult::success(42);\n621\t    /// assert_eq!(success.unwrap_or(0), 42);\n622\t    ///\n623\t    /// let failure = self.value.unwrap_or(default)\n628\t    }\n629\t\n630\t    /// Map the success value to a new type\n631\t    ///\n632\t    /// # Type Parameters\n633\t    /// * `U` - The target type after mapping\n634\t    /// * `F` - Function type that transforms `T` into `U`\n635\t    ///\n636\t    /// # Arguments\n637\t    /// * `f` - A function that transforms the success value\n638\t    ///\n639\t    /// # Returns\n640\t    /// A new `ParseResult<U>` with the transformed value, preserving error, metadata, and warnings\n641\t    ///\n642\t    /// # Examples\n643\t    ///\n644\t    /// ```\n645\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n646\t    ///\n647\t    /// let result: ParseResult<i32> = ParseResult::success(10);\n648\t    /// let doubled = result.map(|x| x * 2);\n649\t    ///\n650\t    /// assert_eq!(doubled.value(), Some(&20));\n651\t    /// ```\n652\t    pub fn map<U, F>(self, f: F) -> ParseResult<U>\n653\t    where\n654\t        F: FnOnce(T) -> U,\n655\t    {\n656\t        match self.value {\n657\t            Some(v) => ParseResult {\n658\t                value: Some(f(v)),\n659\t                error: None,\n660\t                metadata: self.metadata,\n661\t                warnings: self.warnings,\n662\t            },\n663\t            None => ParseResult {\n664\t                value: None,\n665\t                error: self.error,\n666\t                metadata: self.metadata,\n667\t                warnings: self.warnings,\n668\t            },\n669\t        }\n670\t    }\n671\t}\n672\t\n673\timpl<T> From<Result<T>> for ParseResult<T> {\n674\t    fn from(result: Result<T>) -> Self {\n675\t        match result {\n676\t            Ok(value) => Self::success(value),\n677\t            Err(error) => Self::failure(error),\n678\t        }\n679\t    }\n680\t}\n681\t\n682\t/// Metadata about a parsing operation\n683\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n684\tpub struct ParseMetadata {\n685\t    /// Number of lines processed\n686\t    pub DeprecatedField {\n990\t        /// The deprecated field name\n991\t        old_field: String,\n992\t        /// The recommended replacement field\n993\t        new_field: String,\n994\t    },\n995\t\n996\t    /// An unknown key was encountered (in lenient mode)\n997\t    ///\n998\t    /// Indicates a key that was not recognized but was not rejected\n999\t    /// due to lenient parsing }\n1026\t}\n1027\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"da866372-f2a6-4431-8da5-49b83d7e7c4e","uuid":"076c2cda-394b-4790-b0a6-073c5d2d6449","timestamp":"2026-07-13T21:19:01.811Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/src/parsers/yaml/types.rs","content":"//! Result types for YAML parsing operations\n//!\n//! This module defines the result types used by the YAML parser.\n//! This module also integrates error types from bf-68hqo for comprehensive\n//! error categorization and handling.\n\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::error::{ParseError, Result};\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Error type categorization for validation and parsing errors\n///\n/// This enum defines the high-level categories of errors that can occur\n/// during YAML processing and schema validation, following the bf-68hqo\n/// error hierarchy design.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum ErrorType {\n    /// Syntax errors in YAML source (invalid structure, malformed tokens)\n    Syntax,\n    /// I/O errors (file not found, permission denied, read failures)\n    Io,\n    /// Validation errors (constraint violations, required field missing)\n    Validation,\n    /// Type mismatch errors (unexpected type for a field)\n    TypeMismatch,\n    /// UTF-8 encoding errors\n    InvalidUtf8,\n    /// Unexpected end of input\n    UnexpectedEof,\n    /// Unknown anchor or alias references\n    UnknownAnchor,\n    /// Duplicate key in mapping\n    DuplicateKey,\n    /// Other uncategorized errors\n    Other,\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for ErrorType {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::Syntax => write!(f, \"syntax error\"),\n            Self::Io => write!(f, \"I/O error\"),\n            Self::Validation => write!(f, \"validation error\"),\n            Self::TypeMismatch => write!(f, \"type mismatch\"),\n            Self::InvalidUtf8 => write!(f, \"invalid UTF-8 encoding\"),\n            Self::UnexpectedEof => write!(f, \"unexpected end of input\"),\n            Self::UnknownAnchor => write!(f, \"unknown anchor\"),\n            Self::DuplicateKey => write!(f, \"duplicate key\"),\n            Self::Other => write!(f, \"error\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Specific error codes for programmatic error handling\n///\n/// These codes provide machine-readable identifiers for specific error conditions,\n/// enabling automated error handling and reporting. Following the bf-68hqo design,\n/// each code maps to a specific validation or parsing failure scenario.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n#[allow(non_camel_case_types)]\npub enum ErrorCode {\n    // ========== Syntax Errors ==========\n    /// Invalid YAML syntax structure\n    YAML_INVALID_SYNTAX,\n    /// Invalid indentation in YAML\n    YAML_INVALID_INDENTATION,\n    /// Invalid delimiter (colon, bracket, brace)\n    YAML_INVALID_DELIMITER,\n    /// Invalid escape sequence\n    YAML_INVALID_ESCAPE_SEQUENCE,\n    /// Invalid scalar value\n    YAML_INVALID_SCALAR,\n\n    // ========== Type Mismatches ==========\n    /// Expected integer, got another type\n    TYPE_EXPECTED_INTEGER,\n    /// Expected string, got another type\n    TYPE_EXPECTED_STRING,\n    /// Expected boolean, got another type\n    TYPE_EXPECTED_BOOLEAN,\n    /// Expected array, got another type\n    TYPE_EXPECTED_ARRAY,\n    /// Expected object/map, got another type\n    TYPE_EXPECTED_OBJECT,\n    /// Expected number, got another type\n    TYPE_EXPECTED_NUMBER,\n    /// Null value where non-null required\n    TYPE_UNEXPECTED_NULL,\n\n    // ========== Validation Errors ==========\n    /// Required field is missing\n    VALIDATION_REQUIRED_FIELD_MISSING,\n    /// Value out of allowed range\n    VALIDATION_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE,\n    /// String too short\n    VALIDATION_STRING_TOO_SHORT,\n    /// String too long\n    VALIDATION_STRING_TOO_LONG,\n    /// String doesn't match pattern\n    VALIDATION_PATTERN_MISMATCH,\n    /// Value not in allowed set\n    VALIDATION_INVALID_VALUE,\n    /// Array has too few items\n    VALIDATION_ARRAY_TOO_FEW_ITEMS,\n    /// Array has too many items\n    VALIDATION_ARRAY_TOO_MANY_ITEMS,\n    /// Array items not unique\n    VALIDATION_ARRAY_NOT_UNIQUE,\n    /// Object has too few properties\n    VALIDATION_OBJECT_TOO_FEW_PROPERTIES,\n    /// Object has too many properties\n    VALIDATION_OBJECT_TOO_MANY_PROPERTIES,\n\n    // ========== I/O Errors ==========\n    /// File not found\n    IO_FILE_NOT_FOUND,\n    /// Permission denied\n    IO_PERMISSION_DENIED,\n    /// Read failed\n    IO_READ_FAILED,\n    /// Write failed\n    IO_WRITE_FAILED,\n\n    // ========== Encoding Errors ==========\n    /// Invalid UTF-8 sequence\n    ENCODING_INVALID_UTF8,\n\n    // ========== Other Errors ==========\n    /// Unknown anchor reference\n    ANCHOR_UNKNOWN,\n    /// Duplicate key in mapping\n    KEY_DUPLICATE,\n    /// Unexpected end of input\n    EOF_UNEXPECTED,\n}\n\nimpl ErrorCode {\n    /// Get the error type category for this error code\n    pub fn error_type(&self) -> ErrorType {\n        match self {\n            // Syntax errors\n            Self::YAML_INVALID_SYNTAX | Self::YAML_INVALID_INDENTATION |\n            Self::YAML_INVALID_DELIMITER | Self::YAML_INVALID_ESCAPE_SEQUENCE |\n            Self::YAML_INVALID_SCALAR => ErrorType::Syntax,\n\n            // Type mismatches\n            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_INTEGER | Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_STRING |\n            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_BOOLEAN | Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_ARRAY |\n            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_OBJECT | Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_NUMBER |\n            Self::TYPE_UNEXPECTED_NULL => ErrorType::TypeMismatch,\n\n            // Validation errors\n            Self::VALIDATION_REQUIRED_FIELD_MISSING | Self::VALIDATION_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE |\n            Self::VALIDATION_STRING_TOO_SHORT | Self::VALIDATION_STRING_TOO_LONG |\n            Self::VALIDATION_PATTERN_MISMATCH | Self::VALIDATION_INVALID_VALUE |\n            Self::VALIDATION_ARRAY_TOO_FEW_ITEMS | Self::VALIDATION_ARRAY_TOO_MANY_ITEMS |\n            Self::VALIDATION_ARRAY_NOT_UNIQUE | Self::VALIDATION_OBJECT_TOO_FEW_PROPERTIES |\n            Self::VALIDATION_OBJECT_TOO_MANY_PROPERTIES => ErrorType::Validation,\n\n            // I/O errors\n            Self::IO_FILE_NOT_FOUND | Self::IO_PERMISSION_DENIED |\n            Self::IO_READ_FAILED | Self::IO_WRITE_FAILED => ErrorType::Io,\n\n            // Encoding errors\n            Self::ENCODING_INVALID_UTF8 => ErrorType::InvalidUtf8,\n\n            // Other errors\n            Self::ANCHOR_UNKNOWN => ErrorType::UnknownAnchor,\n            Self::KEY_DUPLICATE => ErrorType::DuplicateKey,\n            Self::EOF_UNEXPECTED => ErrorType::UnexpectedEof,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a human-readable description for this error code\n    pub fn description(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            // Syntax errors\n            Self::YAML_INVALID_SYNTAX => \"Invalid YAML syntax structure\",\n            Self::YAML_INVALID_INDENTATION => \"Invalid YAML indentation\",\n            Self::YAML_INVALID_DELIMITER => \"Invalid YAML delimiter\",\n            Self::YAML_INVALID_ESCAPE_SEQUENCE => \"Invalid escape sequence\",\n            Self::YAML_INVALID_SCALAR => \"Invalid scalar value\",\n\n            // Type mismatches\n            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_INTEGER => \"Expected integer type\",\n            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_STRING => \"Expected string type\",\n            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_BOOLEAN => \"Expected boolean type\",\n            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_ARRAY => \"Expected array type\",\n            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_OBJECT => \"Expected object type\",\n            Self::TYPE_EXPECTED_NUMBER => \"Expected number type\",\n            Self::TYPE_UNEXPECTED_NULL => \"Unexpected null value\",\n\n            // Validation errors\n            Self::VALIDATION_REQUIRED_FIELD_MISSING => \"Required field is missing\",\n            Self::VALIDATION_VALUE_OUT_OF_RANGE => \"Value out of allowed range\",\n            Self::VALIDATION_STRING_TOO_SHORT => \"String too short\",\n            Self::VALIDATION_STRING_TOO_LONG => \"String too long\",\n            Self::VALIDATION_PATTERN_MISMATCH => \"Pattern mismatch\",\n            Self::VALIDATION_INVALID_VALUE => \"Invalid value\",\n            Self::VALIDATION_ARRAY_TOO_FEW_ITEMS => \"Array has too few items\",\n            Self::VALIDATION_ARRAY_TOO_MANY_ITEMS => \"Array has too many items\",\n            Self::VALIDATION_ARRAY_NOT_UNIQUE => \"Array items not unique\",\n            Self::VALIDATION_OBJECT_TOO_FEW_PROPERTIES => \"Object has too few properties\",\n            Self::VALIDATION_OBJECT_TOO_MANY_PROPERTIES => \"Object has too many properties\",\n\n            // I/O errors\n            Self::IO_FILE_NOT_FOUND => \"File not found\",\n            Self::IO_PERMISSION_DENIED => \"Permission denied\",\n            Self::IO_READ_FAILED => \"Read failed\",\n            Self::IO_WRITE_FAILED => \"Write failed\",\n\n            // Encoding errors\n            Self::ENCODING_INVALID_UTF8 => \"Invalid UTF-8 encoding\",\n\n            // Other errors\n            Self::ANCHOR_UNKNOWN => \"Unknown anchor reference\",\n            Self::KEY_DUPLICATE => \"Duplicate key in mapping\",\n            Self::EOF_UNEXPECTED => \"Unexpected end of input\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for ErrorCode {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"{:?}\", self)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Status enum representing success/error states for Result types\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum Status {\n    /// Operation completed successfully\n    SUCCESS,\n    /// Operation encountered an error\n    ERROR,\n}\n\nimpl Status {\n    /// Check if status is SUCCESS\n    pub fn is_success(self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Status::SUCCESS)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if status is ERROR\n    pub fn is_error(self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Status::ERROR)\n    }\n\n    /// Convert from boolean (true = SUCCESS, false = ERROR)\n    pub fn from_bool(success: bool) -> Self {\n        if success {\n            Status::SUCCESS\n        } else {\n            Status::ERROR\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Convert to boolean\n    pub fn as_bool(self) -> bool {\n        self.is_success()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for Status {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Status::SUCCESS => write!(f, \"SUCCESS\"),\n            Status::ERROR => write!(f, \"ERROR\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of a YAML operation with status, data, and error fields\n///\n/// This is a simple dataclass-style result type that holds:\n/// - `status`: A Status enum indicating SUCCESS or ERROR\n/// - `data`: Optional generic data payload\n/// - `error`: Optional error message string\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct OperationResult<T> {\n    /// The operation status (SUCCESS or ERROR)\n    pub status: Status,\n    /// The parsed content data, if successful\n    pub data: Option<T>,\n    /// The error message, if the operation failed\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl<T> OperationResult<T> {\n    /// Create a new OperationResult with all three fields\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `status` - The Status enum value (SUCCESS or ERROR)\n    /// * `data` - Optional data payload\n    /// * `error` - Optional error message\n    pub fn new(status: Status, data: Option<T>, error: Option<String>) -> Self {\n        Self { status, data, error }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a successful OperationResult with data\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `data` - The successful result data\n    pub fn success(data: T) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            status: Status::SUCCESS,\n            data: Some(data),\n            error: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a failed OperationResult with an error message\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `message` - The error message describing the failure\n    pub fn error(message: String) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            status: Status::ERROR,\n            data: None,\n            error: Some(message),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the operation was successful\n    pub fn is_success(&self) -> bool {\n        self.status.is_success()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the operation failed\n    pub fn is_error(&self) -> bool {\n        self.status.is_error()\n    }\n\n    /// Get a reference to the data, if successful\n    pub fn get_data(&self) -> Option<&T> {\n        self.data.as_ref()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the error message as a string slice, if failed\n    pub fn get_error(&self) -> Option<&str> {\n        self.error.as_deref()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of a YAML parsing operation\n///\n/// `ParseResult<T>` represents the outcome of a YAML parsing operation, encapsulating\n/// either a successful parse with its associated data and metadata, or a failure with\n/// detailed error information.\n///\n/// # Type Parameter\n///\n/// - `T` - The type of the parsed value. This is typically the target type that the YAML\n///   content is being parsed into (e.g., a configuration struct, `serde_yaml::Value`,\n///   or any deserializable type).\n///\n/// # Fields\n///\n/// ## Core Fields\n///\n/// - `value: Option<T>` - The successfully parsed value. Present when parsing succeeds.\n/// - `error: Option<ParseError>` - Detailed error information. Present when parsing fails.\n/// - `metadata: ParseMetadata` - Metadata about the parsing operation (lines processed,\n///   bytes processed, processing time, source path). Always present.\n/// - `warnings: Vec<ParseWarning>` - Collection of non-fatal warnings that occurred during\n///   parsing. Parsing can succeed with warnings present.\n///\n/// # Design Philosophy\n///\n/// `ParseResult<T>` follows a structured approach to representing parsing outcomes:\n///\n/// 1. **Explicit success/failure states** - Success requires both `value` present and\n///    `error` absent. Failure requires `error` present and `value` absent.\n/// 2. **Rich context** - Both success and failure carry metadata about the operation.\n/// 3. **Warnings without failure** - Warnings are non-fatal; parsing can succeed with\n///    warnings present (e.g., deprecated field usage, unknown keys in lenient mode).\n/// 4. **Composable operations** - Supports `map()` for transforming success values,\n///    and `From<Result<T>>` for interoperability with standard `Result` types.\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ## Successful Parse\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseMetadata};\n///\n/// let result = ParseResult::success(42);\n/// assert!(result.is_success());\n/// assert_eq!(result.value(), Some(&42));\n/// assert!(result.warnings().is_empty());\n/// ```\n///\n/// ## Failed Parse\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseError};\n///\n/// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\");\n/// let result = ParseResult::<i32>::failure(error);\n/// assert!(result.is_failure());\n/// assert!(result.error().is_some());\n/// ```\n///\n/// ## Parse with Warnings\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseWarning};\n///\n/// let mut result = ParseResult::success(42);\n/// result.add_warning(ParseWarning::deprecated_field(\"old_field\", \"new_field\"));\n///\n/// assert!(result.is_success());\n/// assert!(!result.warnings().is_empty());\n/// assert_eq!(result.warnings().len(), 1);\n/// ```\n///\n/// ## Mapping Over Success\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n///\n/// let result: ParseResult<i32> = ParseResult::success(10);\n/// let mapped = result.map(|x| x * 2);\n///\n/// assert_eq!(mapped.value(), Some(&20));\n/// ```\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ParseResult<T> {\n    /// The parsed value, if successful\n    value: Option<T>,\n    /// The error, if parsing failed\n    error: Option<ParseError>,\n    /// Additional metadata about the parse operation\n    metadata: ParseMetadata,\n    /// Non-fatal warnings that occurred during parsing\n    warnings: Vec<ParseWarning>,\n}\n\nimpl<T> ParseResult<T> {\n    /// Create a successful parse result\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `value` - The successfully parsed value\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A `ParseResult` with the value set, no error, empty warnings, and default metadata\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n    ///\n    /// let result = ParseResult::success(42);\n    /// assert!(result.is_success());\n    /// assert_eq!(result.value(), Some(&42));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn success(value: T) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            value: Some(value),\n            error: None,\n            metadata: ParseMetadata::default(),\n            warnings: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a failed parse result\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `error` - The error that caused parsing to fail\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A `ParseResult` with the error set, no value, empty warnings, and default metadata\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseError};\n    ///\n    /// let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\");\n    /// let result = ParseResult::<i32>::failure(error);\n    /// assert!(result.is_failure());\n    /// assert!(result.error().is_some());\n    /// ```\n    pub fn failure(error: ParseError) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            value: None,\n            error: Some(error),\n            metadata: ParseMetadata::default(),\n            warnings: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the parse was successful\n    pub fn is_success(&self) -> bool {\n        self.error.is_none() && self.value.is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the parse failed\n    pub fn is_failure(&self) -> bool {\n        self.error.is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the parsed value\n    ///\n    /// Returns None if the parse failed\n    pub fn value(&self) -> Option<&T> {\n        self.value.as_ref()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the error, if any\n    pub fn error(&self) -> Option<&ParseError> {\n        self.error.as_ref()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the metadata for this parse result\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A reference to the `ParseMetadata` containing information about lines processed,\n    /// bytes processed, processing time, and source path\n    pub fn metadata(&self) -> &ParseMetadata {\n        &self.metadata\n    }\n\n    /// Get the warnings for this parse result\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A slice of `ParseWarning` items representing non-fatal issues that occurred during parsing\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseWarning};\n    ///\n    /// let mut result = ParseResult::success(42);\n    /// result.add_warning(ParseWarning::deprecated_field(\"old\", \"new\"));\n    ///\n    /// assert_eq!(result.warnings().len(), 1);\n    /// ```\n    pub fn warnings(&self) -> &[ParseWarning] {\n        &self.warnings\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this parse result has any warnings\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// `true` if there are warnings present, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn has_warnings(&self) -> bool {\n        !self.warnings.is_empty()\n    }\n\n    /// Add a warning to this parse result\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `warning` - The warning to add\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseWarning};\n    ///\n    /// let mut result = ParseResult::success(42);\n    /// result.add_warning(ParseWarning::unknown_key(\"deprecated_field\"));\n    /// assert!(result.has_warnings());\n    /// ```\n    pub fn add_warning(&mut self, warning: ParseWarning) {\n        self.warnings.push(warning);\n    }\n\n    /// Add warnings to this parse result\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `warnings` - An iterator of warnings to add\n    pub fn add_warnings<I>(&mut self, warnings: I)\n    where\n        I: IntoIterator<Item = ParseWarning>,\n    {\n        self.warnings.extend(warnings);\n    }\n\n    /// Set the metadata for this parse result\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `metadata` - The metadata to set\n    pub fn with_metadata(mut self, metadata: ParseMetadata) -> Self {\n        self.metadata = metadata;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Unwrap the value, consuming the result\n    ///\n    /// # Panics\n    /// Panics if the parse failed (i.e., if `error` is present)\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n    ///\n    /// let result = ParseResult::success(42);\n    /// assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), 42);\n    /// ```\n    pub fn unwrap(self) -> T {\n        self.value.expect(\n            \"called unwrap() on a failed ParseResult\"\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Unwrap the value or return a default\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `default` - The default value to return if parsing failed\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// The parsed value if successful, or the provided default\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseResult, ParseError};\n    ///\n    /// let success: ParseResult<i32> = ParseResult::success(42);\n    /// assert_eq!(success.unwrap_or(0), 42);\n    ///\n    /// let failure = ParseResult::<i32>::failure(ParseError::syntax(\"error\"));\n    /// assert_eq!(failure.unwrap_or(0), 0);\n    /// ```\n    pub fn unwrap_or(self, default: T) -> T {\n        self.value.unwrap_or(default)\n    }\n\n    /// Map the success value to a new type\n    ///\n    /// # Type Parameters\n    /// * `U` - The target type after mapping\n    /// * `F` - Function type that transforms `T` into `U`\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `f` - A function that transforms the success value\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A new `ParseResult<U>` with the transformed value, preserving error, metadata, and warnings\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::ParseResult;\n    ///\n    /// let result: ParseResult<i32> = ParseResult::success(10);\n    /// let doubled = result.map(|x| x * 2);\n    ///\n    /// assert_eq!(doubled.value(), Some(&20));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn map<U, F>(self, f: F) -> ParseResult<U>\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(T) -> U,\n    {\n        match self.value {\n            Some(v) => ParseResult {\n                value: Some(f(v)),\n                error: None,\n                metadata: self.metadata,\n                warnings: self.warnings,\n            },\n            None => ParseResult {\n                value: None,\n                error: self.error,\n                metadata: self.metadata,\n                warnings: self.warnings,\n            },\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<T> From<Result<T>> for ParseResult<T> {\n    fn from(result: Result<T>) -> Self {\n        match result {\n            Ok(value) => Self::success(value),\n            Err(error) => Self::failure(error),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Metadata about a parsing operation\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct ParseMetadata {\n    /// Number of lines processed\n    pub lines_processed: usize,\n    /// Number of bytes processed\n    pub bytes_processed: usize,\n    /// Processing time in nanoseconds\n    pub processing_time_ns: Option<u64>,\n    /// Source file path, if known\n    pub source_path: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl ParseMetadata {\n    /// Create new metadata\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Set the number of lines processed\n    pub fn with_lines(mut self, lines: usize) -> Self {\n        self.lines_processed = lines;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the number of bytes processed\n    pub fn with_bytes(mut self, bytes: usize) -> Self {\n        self.bytes_processed = bytes;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the source path\n    pub fn with_source(mut self, path: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.source_path = Some(path.into());\n        self\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of a YAML validation operation\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ValidationResult {\n    /// Whether validation passed\n    pub valid: bool,\n    /// List of validation errors\n    pub errors: Vec<ValidationError>,\n    /// List of validation warnings\n    pub warnings: Vec<ValidationWarning>,\n}\n\nimpl ValidationResult {\n    /// Create a successful validation result\n    pub fn success() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            valid: true,\n            errors: Vec::new(),\n            warnings: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a failed validation result\n    pub fn failure(errors: Vec<ValidationError>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            valid: false,\n            errors,\n            warnings: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if validation passed\n    pub fn is_valid(&self) -> bool {\n        self.valid && self.errors.is_empty()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if there are any errors\n    pub fn has_errors(&self) -> bool {\n        !self.errors.is_empty()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if there are any warnings\n    pub fn has_warnings(&self) -> bool {\n        !self.warnings.is_empty()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for ValidationResult {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::success()\n    }\n}\n\n/// A validation error\n///\n/// Enhanced with error codes from bf-68hqo for comprehensive error categorization.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ValidationError {\n    /// Path to the invalid element (e.g., \"server.port\")\n    pub path: String,\n    /// Error message\n    pub message: String,\n    /// Machine-readable error code from bf-68hqo hierarchy\n    pub code: ErrorCode,\n    /// Line number where the error occurred (1-indexed)\n    pub line: Option<usize>,\n    /// Column number where the error occurred (1-indexed)\n    pub column: Option<usize>,\n    /// Indentation error type (if this is an indentation error)\n    pub indentation_error_type: Option<crate::parsers::yaml::syntax_detector::IndentationErrorType>,\n    /// Delimiter error type (if this is a delimiter error)\n    pub delimiter_error_type: Option<crate::parsers::yaml::syntax_detector::DelimiterErrorType>,\n}\n\nimpl ValidationError {\n    /// Create a new validation error\n    pub fn new(path: impl Into<String>, message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            path: path.into(),\n            message: message.into(),\n            code: ErrorCode::VALIDATION_INVALID_VALUE, // Default error code\n            line: None,\n            column: None,\n            indentation_error_type: None,\n            delimiter_error_type: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the error code for this validation error\n    pub fn with_code(mut self, code: ErrorCode) -> Self {\n        self.code = code;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the line number for this error\n    pub fn with_line(mut self, line: usize) -> Self {\n        self.line = Some(line);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the column number for this error\n    pub fn with_column(mut self, column: usize) -> Self {\n        self.column = Some(column);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the indentation error type for this error\n    pub fn with_indentation_error_type(mut self, error_type: crate::parsers::yaml::syntax_detector::IndentationErrorType) -> Self {\n        self.indentation_error_type = Some(error_type);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the delimiter error type for this fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match (&self.line, &self.column) {\n            (Some(line), Some(col)) => {\n                write!(f, \"{}:{}: validation error at '{}': {}\", line, col, self.path, self.message)\n            }\n            (Some(line), None) => {\n                write!(f, \"{}: validation error at '{}': {}\", line, self.path, self.message)\n            }\n            (None, Some(col)) => {\n                write!(f, \"col {}: validation error at '{}': {}\", col, self.path, self.message)\n            }\n            (None, None) => {\n                write!(f, \"validation error at '{}': {}\", self.path, self.message)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for ValidationError {}\n\n/// A validation message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            path: path.into(),\n            message: message.into(),\n            line: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the line number fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match &self.line {\n            Some(line) => write!(f, \"{}: warning at '{}': {}\", line, self.path, self.message),\n            None => write!(f, \"warning at '{}': {}\", self.path, self.message),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// A non-fatal warning that occurred during parsing\n///\n/// `ParseWarning` represents issues that don't prevent parsing from completing\n/// but may indicate deprecated usage, potential problems, or other concerns.\n///\n/// # Warning Types\n///\n/// - `DeprecatedField` - A field that has been deprecated and should be migrated\n/// - `UnknownKey` - An unknown key encountered (only in lenient mode)\n/// - `DuplicateKey` - A duplicate key that was handled (lenient mode)\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ParseWarning {\n    /// The kind of warning\n    pub kind: ParseWarningKind,\n    /// Line number where the warning occurred (1-indexed)\n    pub line: Option<usize>,\n}\n\nimpl ParseWarning {\n    /// Create a new parse warning\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `kind` - The type of warning\n    pub fn new(kind: ParseWarningKind) -> Self {\n        Self { kind, line: None }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the line number for this warning\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `line` - The line number (1-indexed)\n    pub fn with_line(mut self, line: usize) -> Self {\n        self.line = Some(line);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Create a deprecated field warning\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `old_field` - The deprecated field name\n    /// * `new_field` - The recommended replacement field\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseWarning, ParseWarningKind};\n    ///\n    /// let warning = ParseWarning::deprecated_field(\"old_api\", \"new_api\");\n    /// assert!(matches!(warning.kind, ParseWarningKind::DeprecatedField { .. }));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn deprecated_field(old_field: impl Into<String>, new_field: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::new(ParseWarningKind::DeprecatedField {\n            old_field: old_field.into(),\n            new_field: new_field.into(),\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Create an unknown key warning\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `key` - The unknown key name\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseWarning, ParseWarningKind};\n    ///\n    /// let warning = ParseWarning::unknown_key(\"unknown_setting\");\n    /// assert!(matches!(warning.kind, ParseWarningKind::UnknownKey(_)));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn unknown_key(key: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::new(ParseWarningKind::UnknownKey(key.into()))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for ParseWarning {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        let location = self.line.map_or_else(|| \"<unknown>\".to_string(), |l| l.to_string());\n        write!(f, \"{}: {}\", location, self.kind)\n    }\n}\n\n/// The kind of parse warning\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub enum ParseWarningKind {\n    /// A deprecated field was used\n    ///\n    /// Indicates that a field has been deprecated and should be replaced\n    DeprecatedField {\n        /// The deprecated field name\n        old_field: String,\n        /// The recommended replacement field\n        new_field: String,\n    },\n\n    /// An unknown key was encountered (in lenient mode)\n    ///\n    /// Indicates a key that was not recognized but was not rejected\n    /// due to lenient parsing mode\n    UnknownKey(String),\n\n    /// A duplicate key was encountered (in lenient mode)\n    ///\n    /// Indicates a duplicate key that was handled by taking one of the values\n    DuplicateKey(String),\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for ParseWarningKind {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        match self               old_field, new_field\n                )\n            }\n            Self::UnknownKey(key) => {\n                write!(f, \"warning: unknown key '{}'\", key)\n            }\n This module provides the core data structures for representing parsed YAML lines.\n4\t//! It defines types that hold line-by-line parsing results, including line type,\n5\t//! indentation level, and raw content.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! ## Architecture\n8\t//!\n9\t//! The line parser module provides the foundational types for line-based YAML analysis:\n10\t//!\n11\t//! - [`LineType`] - Enumeration of all possible YAML line types\n12\t//! - [`YamlLine`] - Struct representing a single parsed YAML line\n13\t//! - [`LineContent`] - Structured content representation for different line types\n14\t//!\n15\t//! ## Usage\n16\t//!\n17\t//! ```ignore\n18\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::line_parser::{YamlLine, LineType};\n19\t//!\n20\t//! let line = YamlLine::new(1, \"key: value\", 0, LineType::MappingKey);\n21\t//! assert_eq!(line.line_number(), 1);\n22\t//! assert_eq!(line.raw_content(), \"key: value\");\n23\t//! ```\n24\t//!\n25\t//! ## Line Type Categories\n26\t//!\n27\t//! - **Structural**: Blank lines, document markers\n28\t//! - **Comments**: Full and inline comments\n29\t//! - **Content**: Mapping keys, values, sequence items\n30\t//! - **Special**: Anchors, aliases, tags, directives\n31\t\n32\tuse std::fmt;\n33\t\n34\t/// Classification of YAML line types\n35\t///\n36\t/// This enum provides structured categorization of different line types\n37\t/// that can occur in YAML files. Each variant represents a specific semantic\n38\t/// category recognized by the YAML specification.\n39\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n40\tpub enum LineType {\n41\t    /// Empty line patterns)\n87\t    Unknown,\n88\t}\n89\t\n90\timpl LineType {\n91\t    /// Get a human-readable description of this line type\n92\t    pub fn description(&self) -> &'static str {\n93\t        match self {\n94\t            Self::Blank => \"blank line\",\n95\t            Self::Comment => \"comment line\",\n96\t            Self::DocumentStart => \"document start marker\",\n97\t            Self::DocumentEnd => \"document end marker\",\n98\t            Self::MappingKey => \"mapping key\",\n99\t            Self::MappingValue => \"mapping value\",\n100\t            Self::SequenceItem => \"sequence item\",\n101\t            Self::FlowMapping => \"flow style mapping\",\n102\t            Self::FlowSequence => \"flow style sequence\",\n103\t            Self::Anchor => \"anchor definition\",\n104\t            Self::Alias => \"alias reference\",\n105\t            Self::Tag => \"tag directive\",\n106\t            Self::Directive => \"YAML directive\",\n107\t            Self::LiteralBlockScalar => \"literal block scalar\",\n108\t            Self::FoldedBlockScalar => \"folded block scalar\",\n109\t            Self::Unknown => \"unknown content\",\n110\t        }\n111\t    }\n112\t\n113\t    /// Check if this line type is a structural element\n114\t    ///\n115\t    /// Structural elements are blank lines, document markers, and comments\n116\t    /// that don't contribute to the actual data content.\n117\t    pub fn is_structural(&self) -> bool {\n118\t        matches!(\n119\t            self,\n120\t            Self::Blank | Self::DocumentStart | Self::DocumentEnd | Self::Comment\n121\t        )\n122\t    }\n123\t\n124\t    /// Check if this line type is meaningful content\n125\t    ///\n126\t    /// Meaningful content includes keys, values, and data items.\n127\t    pub fn is_content(&self) -> bool {\n128\t        matches!(\n129\t            self,\n130\t            Self::MappingKey |\n131\t            Self::MappingValue |\n132\t            Self::SequenceItem |\n133\t            Self::FlowMapping |\n134\t            Self::FlowSequence |\n135\t            Self::LiteralBlockScalar |\n136\t            Self::FoldedBlockScalar\n137\t        )\n138\t    }\n139\t\n140\t    /// Check if this line type is a YAML directive or special marker\n141\t    pub fn is_directive(&self) -> bool {\n142\t        matches!(self, Self::Directive | Self::Tag | Self::Anchor | Self::Alias)\n143\t    }\n144\t\n145\t    /// Get all line type variants\n146\t    pub fn all() -> &'static [Self] {\n147\t        &[\n148\t            Self::Blank,\n149\t Self::SequenceItem,\n155\t            Self::FlowMapping,\n156\t            Self::FlowSequence,\n157\t            Self::Anchor,\n158\t            Self::Alias,\n159\t            Self::Tag,\n160\t fmt::Display for LineType {\n169\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n170\t        write!(f, \"{}\", self.description())\n171\t    }\n172\t}\n173\t\n174\t/// Structured data.\n178\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n179\tpub enum LineContent {\n180\t    /// No content (blank lines, document markers)\n181\t    Empty,\n182\t\n183\t    /// Comment content (text after #)\n184\t    Comment(String),\n185\t\n186\t    /// Mapping key content (key name and optional value)\n187\t    MappingKey {\n188\t        /// The key name\n189\t        key: String,\n190\t        /// The value, if    /// Alias reference\n213\t    Alias {\n214\t        /// The alias name\n215\t        name: String,\n216\t    },\n217\t\n218\t    /// Tag directive\n219\t    Tag {\n220\t        /// The tag
